What is the attractive character?
And by the way, Russell and his staff were kind enough to pass out the slides of the presentation I'm about to give you so that you can follow along and take notes and write stuff down as I'm going through it.
So that'll be very helpful.
All right.
What is the attractive character?
The attractive character is a voice in which you communicate to your customers and your prospects via all media.
The more attractive your voice is, the more responsive your list becomes.
That's a real important thing to keep in mind.
If you're dull, deadly, and boring, like most marketers are, they're going to turn you off no matter how good your content is.
Content is not king.
It's important.
You got to deliver good content.
But the attractive character, the personality that you give to people is more important.
If you think back to when you were in college or high school or grammar school, you don't remember the teachers that were dull, deadly, and boring.
They're kind of all blurred together.
But I'll bet every single one of you has a teacher, a professor, a coach, gunnery sergeant for some of you, a officer that you remember because they were almost a larger than life personality and you learned more from them and retained more from them than you did from anyone else.
And the reason for that is we are all biologically programmed to respond to people that exhibit characteristics like that.
And so the more interesting, fun, engaging, controversial, attractive you are in your marketing, i.e. the further away you get from the dull, deadly, boring, antiseptic corporate environment that so many people are used to, the more responsive your list becomes.
And the more responsive your list becomes and the more they feel they have a relationship with you, the more likely they are to give you money, which is why I hope we're all here, to get money.

And so these, the most successful information marketing businesses that you see are built around this concept of the attractive character.
And this attractive character is deliberately planned, deliberately engineered, and there's a lot of things that go into it to create the relationship with the customer list.
When I started my business, I started my business on my women approach you business on May 23rd of 2004.
And before I sat down and even wrote a sales letter or did an email, I thought to myself, what kind of character can I create so that I can bond with my customers?
And I wrote it out very carefully, you know, what I'm going to call myself, what I'm going to say, what I'm going to stand for, what I'm not going to stand for, to engage the people to read.
And it was very, very successful.
So I called myself the king of let him come to you.
And there was even a lot of thought that went into that name, as I'll talk about as we go through this.
When somebody offsends one of your lists, when somebody buys from you, especially if you're an information model, that they're looking to you as an authority, okay, as an expert.
And therefore, they expect you to behave and interact with them as an expert, okay, as an authority.
If you don't, they get a little bit nervous.
I mean, have you ever been around somebody that, you know, maybe you thought that they weren't experts on something, they weren't an authority, when you met them in person, you know, they were kind of weak, mild, a little bit wimpy.
And you got a little bit nervous, because there was a disconnect, there was an incongruency.
And so it's a very important thing, is that when you're designing your own character, is that it's from a position of authority, okay, kind of like the, you know, the uncle that cares about you, you know, or the tough old guy with the heart of gold that's going to get the best out of you, is that people look towards you as an authority and expect you to behave in a certain manner.
So I call myself the king, okay, of let them come to you.
And I did that very deliberately.
One king is, implies authority, let them come to you, is probably the unique selling proposition in my business, how to get Wilmington to approach you for a date, how to get Wilmington to approach you first for a date, no matter your book's age or income.
That's my unique selling proposition, okay.
And so I put that into the attractive character.
If I call myself the peasant of make them run away fast, it wouldn't have been so good, right.
Doesn't quite work, all right.
And so a lot of deliberate thought goes into creating this attractive character that you then begin to wrap your business around and communicate with your customers in a two-way fashion.
Well, really kind of a one and a half-way fashion, because here's what I mean by that, is that I have a lot of dialogue with my email list.
I do two emails a date on my list, I'm going to talk about that strategy as we go along.
So I get a lot of talkback stuff.
I encourage my customers to write in, I encourage them to respond to me, okay, because it kind of creates a community.
So if they put something in and I like what it is they have to say, then I'll reprint it in an email and give my comments.
I do not communicate directly with them one-on-one unless they paid me a very significant amount of money.
Why is this?
This is a line I got from Dan Kennedy, the guru does not sit at the bottom of the hill, okay.
Guru sits at the top of the hill.
You have to work to get to the guru, okay.
And if, and you know you're doing this right, is if you accidentally respond to an email or something and you get an email immediately back from it says, oh my god, I never thought I'd expect to hear from you personally.
Oh my god, thank you for responding.

And so you want them to feel like they have access to you but only as a group, all right, because that's the way an important person behaves.

Now, how do they do that?
They pay you lots and lots and lots and lots of money to get access to that, okay, that's how.

The other interesting thing about that is, is what that does for you, is it lets you know that, hey, this is a real person writing to me, okay, they're writing, they sat down and wrote a really email to me, this isn't some canned auto responder that, you know, it's another internet marketer put on his A-web or auto responder, and I'm getting number 27 of 365 this year.
This is a real guy or a real woman writing this to me, because they reference something that happened, okay, and so they know, hey, this is a real person that took the time to sit down and write something to me.
Now I'm going to get into the mechanics of this two emails a day strategy, or better yet, probably what would be called it is, it is large frequency of communication, because it's not just an email, it's in all other media as well.
I want to make that very clear, but the point that I want to make is, is that they know it's you that's writing to them, that you took a little bit of time.
Now this doesn't take a lot of time to do, I do my two emails that I send out every day in 15 minutes for two of them, okay, but it is important that they know that there's a real person talking to them.
Another thing that they have to know is that you have, you know, some certain quirks and beliefs and stands and positions that you'll take a position of advocacy on things, and in my list I'm teaching men how to attract women, and so I have some very strong, very definite opinions on some of the things that happen in culture and society, and so I comment on them.
One thing, it's kind of a personal thing to me, but one thing that I absolutely abhorred and detest is what happened musically since 1992, okay, Rock and Roll died in 1992, right, I'm a big 80s metal fan, because that's when I grew up, when there were bands, you know, larger than life, attractive character bands like Motley Crue and Losp, and you know, all those guys like that, that ruled the stage, and women were after them, and all that went away, and men became wimpy, woosy, and soft in 1992 when their barnor showed up and killed music, okay.
Now, to a lot of people here in the room listening, that may not mean anything, but to my list it means a lot, because it's, hey, here used to be the Rock and Roll attitude that was very attractive to women, now we've had this big cultural shift where guys are walking down looking at the feet, mumbling, whining, full of angst because they were born into prosperity.
Oh, how terrible, and and so I make it a big point in my emails, you know, to talk about that, you know, I'll go say, hey, look, you know, there used to be this home by this band called Twisted Sister, a little bit goofy, but they had songs like, we're not gonna take it, or we're gonna make it, or shoot them down, or something like that, and then you get songs today with some guy whining that, oh, you're so much better than me, or so, she's so high above me, oh, give me a break, you know, I mean, I can't stand it, it drives me crazy, and so that's a very authentic thing that I share with my list, and I actually am pleased to see that I get a lot of young guys come back to, hey, John, you know, you're right, these bands today, they're horrible, they really suck, give me a list of bands to go listen to, here you go, fellas go listen to these, go do your homework, go get some of the old videos, watch how they perform, watch how they walk, watch how they move, watch how they carry themselves, you know, they're not mumbling, looking at the ground, their shoulders slumped and all this, I mean, they're out there larger than life, and they're attractive, okay, and so that's one thing that I share with my list, it's a personal thing, but it touches the nerve with a lot of the guys on my list, and it is relevant to the topic at hand, okay, I do want to make that point, okay, at times you can go off on a tangent, but you never want to go so far off that you're getting away from the core topic of what you're talking about, okay, and so what I'll always do is I may make a comment to say something, but I'll always bring it back to the core topic at hand, and I may put it in my emails, okay, how does what I just say have to do with how to attract and keep the woman of your dreams, okay, here's how it does, and then I'll give some content, and so an important part of the formula is tell a short story, teach a lesson, tell a short story, teach a lesson, okay, and so you get them in, and you get them engaged with the story, and then what you do is you teach them the lesson, by the way, if you want to see a really interesting psychological thing, next time you're around a group of people, a group of friends, everybody's talking, nobody's listening to each other, and you want them to shut up, all you got to do is say, hey, let me tell you a story, everybody shuts up and listens and looks at you because, hey, we love stories, we want to hear something interesting, somebody's got a story to tell, I want to hear it, you know, and so the better storyteller you become, the more you get your list to shut up and listen to you, okay, and if they're listening to you, that's when you're able to sell them something, okay, and so telling stories, especially sharing personal parts of your life, is a real important thing.
Some other things that I share, I'm a very big believer in physical fitness, I mean, I spend a lot of money on, you know, supplements and standing shape and working out and all that type of stuff, and again, that's something that is something that I believe in very much, but it's something that's relevant to the topic at hand.
Yes, I say my unique selling proposition is how to get them to approach you first, no matter your looks, age, or income, and you can do that, however, if you've done something about your looks, you've done something about your income, may not be able to do anything about your age or your hair, but you can do something about that, then you have a much better chance of success, and so it ties into it, physical fitness.
The other thing that that does that's very interesting is it also sets up the sale of other products to my list, may not be directly related to the subject at hand, but now I'm able to go out to like a friend of mine, Ryan Lee, who runs membership besides about fitness, and say, hey, Ryan, you want to do a joint venture?
Hey, I got a whole, you know, group of guys that they need to get to butts in shape, okay, so why don't we do a deal, and so they're responsive to that, or my friend Tim Larkin, I don't know if you know Tim, he teaches target focused training, Tim's a good guy, he hates the word self-defense, but it's self-defense.
What it is, it's kind of a fighting system, and I think it's a really good thing, it's how to protect yourself, you know, when the situation goes, so that ties in too, so I did a joint venture with Tim Larkin, and we sold a bunch of the systems to my list, because I'm able to go in and talk about relevant, go in and talk about topics that are indirectly related, and I have personal beliefs and stories about, but then I set them up for the sale of other related products, which I don't have to create, so that's good.
The other thing too is that I occasionally talk about, you know, my military background, I was, you know, unfortunately, I went to school at the University of Texas on a four-year naval ROTC scholarship, I was selected to be a submarine officer, Nuke Power, and three months before commissioning, they found a problem with my eyes, so why you can't be a naval officer, you got to go be a civilian, so, you know, I got that story to tell too, you know, and I had the time that I spent out on the fleet during the summer, and so what that does is it allows me to form a bond with some of the current active duty military guys on my list, and also form a bond with some of the people that may be in 30 years ago, and so when you let out, when you let out these particular different sides of yourself, what you're doing is, is you're creating a bond with different segments of your list, okay, and so, you know, the guy that's a civilian and has never had anything to do with military service, may not care about the fact that I was in the Navy, you may not care at all, but he may really like what I have to say about 80s music versus the gallblrids that came after, okay, and then you got somebody that's, you know, may not care about either of those, but they're really big into physical fitness, they're really big into martial arts, and so what I'm able to do is, is I'm able to form a relationship with different segments of my list to get them to pay attention to what it is that I have to say, so what you may want to do is sit down and write, make a big list of all the stuff that you yourself are passionate about, that's interesting about you, that you believe in, so that you begin to let these things out to your list to form a relationship with them.
The important thing is, it's, it's, it's that you've got to be authentic, that you can't take something that you don't believe in, that you don't care about, and try to make it part of your attractive, that doesn't work, people see right through it.
What makes this, what makes this so powerful is the fact is that you are authentic, okay, and I know that's a word that like Foreman asked, has stolen and you know, kind of used a lot, and you know, but, but if you look at the true meaning of that word, it's a really good way to describe it, you want to be real, you want to be genuine, you want to be authentic, okay, you also want to be passionate, you want to be authoritative, you want to do the types of things, you want to be exciting, you want to do the types of things that create a bond with your list, it's very important.
All right, talked about that.
It's important to speak in one voice throughout all media, and here's what I mean by that, is not just an email, but the closer people get to you, is that you want to continue to be the same guy, and so if you talk with me one-on-one, if you talk with me in a mastermind group, if you talk to me personally, you're going to get what you're getting from the stage.
Now, I may occasionally let a few four-letter words drop in private that I would never say in public.
No, not at all, absolutely not.
Working eight months in the oil field and then in the Navy and stuff, I never heard a word of anything like that.
Certainly not from gunnery sergeant, hostire, any of those other fine individuals that I met while I was out.
But other than that, is that I'm genuine and authentic all the way through.
The people that drive me crazy the most, and they drive most people crazy the most, are the guys and women that put themselves out as one thing, but by the behavior and their actions turn out to be another.
Then people get wind of that, especially on the internet, that they're a phony, they're a fraud, they're not doing business with, you don't want to be around them, okay, and therefore you don't want to have a personal relationship with them.
Remember, the important thing about this attractive character is wanting people to bond with you and get closer to you, because the more they want to get closer to you, the more money they're willing to pay for access.
I mean, that's one reason why I pay Kennedy so much money, is that I'm able to pay for access, but also get the unfettered Kennedy, which has been helpful to my business, but which is also personally entertaining to me.
And so that's a very important thing to keep in mind.
The more that you bond with them, and the more that you're congruent and authentic all the way through your communications with them, the more responsive they're going to be, the longer they're going to stick around, and the more money that they're going to give you.
You want to say things that other people are thinking, but don't have the guts to say themselves.
Howard Stern of all people is somewhat of a master of this.
I'm not necessarily a Stern fan, not because I don't like the guys, just I listen to them for a week and it's like, oh, I can't take it anymore.
But that's just me.
A lot of people find Howard Stern very attractive, and Sirius Sallat-Royal giving him $30 million to move his listeners over is certainly indicative of that.
But one thing that makes Howard Stern such an attractive character is he will say things that other people are thinking and wish they could say, but don't have the guts to say.
And so that's one thing to keep in mind that's really, really important.
I'll come out and say, I have no problem saying it in my list.
I think there's a problem with today's man.
All these younger guys walking around shuffling their feet, I think there's a real problem with them.
And I think there's a problem in society in how they treat these young guys and what they're expecting to do.
And I think there's a problem in society and culture in the media.
And a lot of people are afraid to say that because it's not politically correct.
I have no problem saying that.
However, it is also relevant to my list.
I'm not going off and talking about, to my list, why we should be in Iraq or not should be in Iraq, because although I may have opinions on that, not relevant to my list.
But on society and culture and how young men are being treated and why most of today's women cannot stand today's men, very relevant.
And so I'll say what other men are afraid to say.
And it creates a bond.
Another point I want to make about Howard Stern for a second is this.
Remember where I said that content is not king, entertainment is.
Well, he's a perfect example of that.
Because I don't think in the whole history of time he's been on the radio, he's uttered one word of content.
Not one.
I listened to the guy, I didn't make no money off of him.
Except for paying attention to his attractive character, but from no content.
And directly maybe, but no.
But he got $30 million to go over to serious satellite radio because of the entertainment and because of the people that are drawn to him to listen to what it is that he has to say, even if it's nothing.
Okay.
And he's really good at saying nothing a variety of different ways throughout the whole time of his career, over and over and over.
He said nothing enough times that people will come in and listen to nothing and be happy for it.
They'll even pay now to listen to nothing.
All right.
So it's an important thing to keep in mind.
But listen to Stern is actually a very good exercise in developing your attractive character.
Okay.
Controversial, that kind of goes along with what I just said, is that you do want to be controversial, but you want to be ethnically and genuinely controversial.
You don't want to be deliberately controversial.
And so I know guys that go out and they'll try and create controversy about something that they really care nothing about one way or the other.
Well, that's not very genuine and people tend to pick up on that.
And so if you're going to be controversial, do it in a way where you're saying something strong about something that you believe in that's relevant to your list.
This is this last one here is very important is that you don't deliberately seek to offend anyone.
But you're not afraid to offend anyone by speaking strongly on a subject that you're passionate about.
I can go out and offend my whole list tomorrow and they're never going to come back.
Okay.
I can say all kinds of mean, nasty, hateful, hurtful things.
That's not what I mean by not being afraid to offend somebody.
You don't ever want to set out to deliberately offend someone.
But what you stand for, what you had to say, what you're passionate about, how you defend your position, and invariably will offend somebody and you should welcome that instead of being afraid of it.
I've known guys, this drives me a little bit crazy, but they sent out something to their list and 100,000 people on their list and they got three complaints.
Well, my message to the complainers is I don't care what you have to say, you know, I don't have to say get off my list and unsubscribe link below.
And really the truth is we pay attention to people who give us money, not the people who don't give us money.
But I've known marketers that have gotten three complaints like that and this sound an apology letter the next day.
Oh my god, I'm sorry I did this.
Well, that's the best way to kill the list because you were afraid to offend.
You really didn't mean what you said the day before.
All right.
And so you cannot be afraid to offend people by your positions and what you have to say.
But you don't want to set out to seek to deliberately offend somebody.
And nor do you want to do stuff to the point where it is so offensive that it really hurts your business.
Because remember what we're all here for is the getting and keeping of money.
Okay.
I'll give you an example.
This is something that I tested a couple of years ago.
I actually sent something.
It was when it was when Bill Clinton was in the hospital getting his heart transplant or whatever came from running and eating McDonald's Big Macs at the same time.
Is that he was in the hospital and I can't actually write about Bill Clinton because if you're a guy that wants to learn how to attract women, whatever you think about him personally or politically, you better pay attention to that man.
Because one thing he knows how to do is attract women.
So I wrote an email about, yeah, Bill's in the hospital.
I know where that hag Hillary's away is that, you know, Bill's, he's talking with those nurses.
I know Bill's talking to the nurses.
And I got a lot of surprisingly negative email and opt-outs about how dare you say that about Hillary.
And Bill's a beloved fellow in this and that.
Oh, I mean, I'm not going to comment about that again because it was too much.
I wanted to try it and see what happened, but it was too much.
Okay.
And so now we're not talking about that hag Hillary.
I do still occasionally talk about Bill, but in the light of, hey, here's the subject matter.
Here's why you need to pay attention to him.
But I know Bill was talking to those nurses while he was in the hospital.
I'd have been doing the same thing too.
Okay.
Another important part, point is, really glad you had some handouts here, is that you want to know, is that your list needs to know what you stand for in your philosophy.
Okay.
Is that you want to have a consistent philosophy you follow.
One of my philosophies that I conveyed in my list is, it's all your fault.
Okay.
Everything that happens to you is your fault even if you think you have no control over it because it's a good mindset to have.
If your wife left you, it's your fault.
If your girlfriend dumped you, it's your fault.
Because what that means is, is that you engage in some behavior that created that situation.
And if it's your fault, that's really good news because it means that it's in your control.
So you change the behavior, you get a different outcome.
Because one thing that I sell against quite a bit are a lot of people that have an entitlement mentality, a victim mentality, guys that say, oh, women just don't like me for who I am.
Well, duh.
Who would like you for who you are?
Yes, she is so much better than you.
And no, she does not want to be with you for that reason.
Okay.
But I convey a consistent philosophy of if you want to get results, it's all your fault, which is good news because it means that that it's your behavior is in your control.
You can change your behavior, change your behavior, you get results.
Now something very interesting about this, it's all your fault philosophy too.
It's not only does this apply to the subject of attraction, it also applies to the subject of making money.
Okay.
Now actually, I'll tell you what the parallel is.
If you look at what I teach and how to get these guys to attract women, you take that and you take what's taught in a direct marketing course, you're going to see 90% of the stuff is the same.
Attraction is attraction is attraction.
How you attract women is the same way you attract money.
Okay.
Sometimes if you attract money, you'll also attract women at the same time.
But that's a different syndrome.
But and so what I'm able to do with that philosophy that that that I'm able to in general put out to people is I'm also able to say, hey, this part of this philosophy is the core thing we're here about.
But there's this other section here, this making money stuff, which is really cool too.
And by the way, I got a really good course you need to check out.
You might be surprised to hear, but I actually get some pretty decent affiliate commission checks from the laser Kennedy.
I get some pretty decent affiliate commission checks from internet marketing center, Derek Gale's office.
Okay, both of those are about making money.
But the reason that I'm able to get those checks is as I'm able to say, hey, guys, what's going on with these individuals is consistent with the philosophy that I teach you of.
It's all your fault.
Okay, your relationship situation is your fault.
But so is your financial situation.
Okay, now we showed you how to fix your relationship situation.
Now you go follow what these guys have to say, fix your financial situation too.
And so when you have an overriding philosophy like that, not only does it attract people to you, but it also sets up the sale of indirectly related items.
Okay.
And so the very interesting thing about my business is is I may only get customers in at the front end and a pretty decent cost to sale, but then also back in them other products that are not directly related.
If you've ever played in the money making or the internet marketing industry and tried to front end and get new customers, you know, without having without a joint venture, getting or having a relationship with somebody, it's really difficult to do.
There's a lot of competition out there.
It's hard to do.
But because I'm able to get my customers in a much lower cost for a different market, because of the relationship I form with them, now I'm able to sell them other things, internet marketing and money making stuff, that's all profit to me because I didn't have to put out the huge front end cost of getting the customer in the first place.
And so that's what a consistent philosophy does is it allows you to set up the sale of indirectly related products.
Okay, especially if you have a customer list.
I go to any internet marketer, if I could get them to build a good relationship with their list, I know for an absolute fact, because I've done it with a few among a small level that I that could sell dating and relationship stuff.
The philosophy of hey, it's all your fault, we're getting your income situation fixed.
But hey, you may have a relationship situation you need fixed, we'll talk with this guy, John.
Okay, it's a very powerful thing because now you don't have to create a bunch of products to unlock the value in your list.
All you have to do is go find different joint ventures.
Another important part is that you like to have fun.
And above all, you're entertaining and you're never boring.
Okay, the number one sin of marketing is being boring.
It's the ultimate marketing sin.
And you really have to guard against that.
Even I sometimes read my email, so I'm getting a little boring, I need something to spice it up to keep renters to get excited.
So I'll tell them a story.
But that's one thing you have to guard against all your marketing communication, especially in this day and age.
Because here's what being interesting and not being boring will do for you.
If you go look at your email inbox, you're going to see there's a mountain of spam.
If you go to your mailbox, you're going to see that there's probably a mountain of direct mail.
If you check your voicemail messages, there's going to be you're just everybody today is oversaturated with information.
The way that you cut through the clutter and you get your message open and your message read is being more interesting and exciting than all the other messages that are there.
Okay, and so you want people to anticipate and look forward to hearing from you so that you'll get your message open.
If you don't get your message open, you won't get your message read.
Okay.
And so that's a really important part of this whole attractive character thing is that it cuts through the clutter.
The more exciting you are, the more you're going to cut through it.
Okay.
And so I mean, I get I know guys, it's interesting.
Every now and then I'll get somebody.
I actually had a guy print out a couple years ago.
He printed out he was sending me an order, but he printed out his entire it was his whitelist.
It was he had all these it was all his outlook folders.
He had all these folders from all these different people that he received email from all these different newsletters.
I recognize them.
I mean, this thing was like two pages long, and he had all these things.
And I guarantee you that half those things he never went in and read, but he went in and read mine because it was interesting and exciting.
And then he sent me a check.
Okay.
And so that's the real important thing about developing this exciting dynamic, fun, attractive character is it cuts through and it gets it gets your mail open and it gets it read.
The other thing too, now we're going to start getting into the mechanics of this frequency of communication strategy is with all the spam filters and stuff out there now, it's a lot it's it's hard to get email delivered.
It's really hard to get email delivered.
Okay.
But if you're writing to somebody like you would talk to them, like you're going to talk to a friend of yours, you're writing to them in that manner, it's a conversational one on one thing.
The email more likely than not is going to get through, because the spam filters look at it like they would a piece of personal communication.
And it is a piece of personal communication between you and 100,000 of your best prospects customers.
Okay.
But it is.
And so it tends to get through, it can get treated as and viewed by the people on the other end is a personal communication.
Okay.
And they're going to look at you just like they're going to look at their friend Joe that just sent them for the upteenth time the video of the monkey scratching is behind.
You know, they're going to look at you the same thing because it's open, it's exciting.
It's what does this guy John have to say today?
Well, either I love him, I hate him, but I got to see what he has to say because he's intriguing and exciting.
Okay.
And so that's, that's what the attractive character does about being entertaining and boring.
One other technique and then we're going to get into one other thing.
I'll get in some email specifics.
This is a real important one is that you want to in your list or your community is you want to have an enemy.
Doesn't matter who the enemy is, but it needs to be an us versus them.
Okay.
Because people naturally split themselves into two camps and us versus them.
And so the more that you position yourself as a member and leader of the us since and sell against them, they're going to pay attention to you.
Okay.
And so what I do is I split my world into talkers and doers.
Okay.
And anybody that behaves in a manner that I don't like, like they whine or, you know, they freeload or something like that, they're a talker.
They don't take action.
They don't buy anything.
Okay.
Guys that go out, take action, get results, semi testimonials, buy more stuff, they're doers.
Okay.
And it's natural.
And it's, it's naturally along, you know, lines to it's a philosophical thing, but I always rail about talkers and doers.
Okay.
Is that, oh, this guy sent me this email whining that he didn't, that, that it didn't get any results for him.
He's nothing but a talker and you don't want to be a talker.
Okay.
And then I'll get an email from a guy, say, Hey, John, I got your system went out, took action.
Here's the results that it got.
Man, this stuff is great.
Can't wait for your best product.
I'll send it out and say, look at this doer.
He went out and he took action.
He got results.
This is the type of doer that you should be.
He's a good guy.
Okay.
So let me hear from all you other doers.
And when you do this enough, what I'll get sometimes is from guys, if I release a new product, John, thanks for your new offer.
I mean, I can't buy it now, you know, but I'm going to buy it soon.
I want to let you know that I'm not one of those talkers you talk about.
I'm a doer.
And I'm a buy.
Okay, that's good.
I mean, that's just doer thinking.
And so what you encourage is this us versus them.
Okay.
Now, it doesn't matter who the us and the them is.
I mean, I just kind of made that up.
But if you look in, in different niches, there's a natural us versus them, you know, you're looking to diet niche, it's us versus the food companies that want to get us fat.
You know, if you're looking like the insurance niche, it's us agents versus the idiots at the home office.
There's always an us versus them that you can find.
And you want to begin to weave that into your communications.
It's very, very, very important.
Because if we have a common enemy, we unite together to fight that common enemy.
If we don't, we're not nearly as responsive.
Okay, it's just, it's a facet of human nature and something that you should pay attention to.
So whatever it is, okay, like, you know, real estate investing, well, those realtors are the enemy.
Banks, banks are the enemy.
Okay, so there's lots of enemies out there.
In internet marketing, who's the enemy and who's the big friend at the same time?
Google.
You know, to a lot of internet marketers that evil Google came and slapped us all and, you know, and now we're not able to get cheap paperclip traffic and, you know, run.
Well, I'm not gonna mention that.
But anyways.
But so there's a natural enemy there.
Or in internet marketing, one of the enemies is the gurus.
You know, a lot of people see there's all these gurus out there with these secrets that they're not sharing that are making all this money and taking us from poor little guys who, you know, are good little internet marketers that spend a lot of times whining on the forums.
And so that these guys are the reason we're broke.
Okay, whether or not that's true has nothing to do with anything.
The fact is, is the particular belief, and it's an us versus them.
And so in each market and each category that you're in, there's always an us versus them to play upon.
And it really focuses people because people love to be part of a group, even in this day and age, and they want to be part of something special.
The us versus them gives an opportunity for them to do that.
If we have time at the end, I'll show you an interesting exercise, but I want to get through to some of the specifics first.
All right, let's get on to this two emails a day strategy.
Okay.
Now, I really should retitle this, the frequency of communication strategy, because I don't want everybody to think that this is an email-only technique.
I don't want everybody to think that they should rely only on email.
The things I'm going to teach you are, as viable in email print, fax, tele-seminar, newsletter, any method of media.
It just so happens that, you know, if you're an internet marketer, one of the best ways to get started, and one of the best tools that you use is email.
And this, you know, to give credit, as credit is due, is, you know, somehow I was clued in on in 2004 by Matt Fury at a dinner that, you know, we had in Phoenix.
And he learned about this strategy from Agora Publishing that does their early to rise.
And because the deal before always had been with internet marketers, it was a conventional wisdom was you can do one email a week to your list.
One must be content only, and one must be sales only.
And if you do more than one email a week, they will opt out, and they will not like you.
Well, I mean, Matt was listening to the early to rise, which is from Agora.
Agora is a big company.
They're an old-time direct response marketing company.
They mail a million direct mail pieces a month.
These dudes know what they're talking about.
And the guy says email your list two times a day.
So Matt emails his list two times a day.
Dramatically increases sales.
So he tells me about that.
So I'm going to email my list two times a day.
I don't have a list yet, but when I do, I'm going to email two times a day.
The three people on it, there's going to be six emails going out.
All right?
So Matt was the one that clued me in on this, and he does a really good job with this.
Now, the caveat is to this, is this only works in the voice of the attractive character.
If you are dull, deadly, and boring, the old-time internet marketers are absolutely right.
People will not like you, and they will leave.
If you try and sell them or pursue to be selling them something every single day with not giving them any entertainment, then they're going to leave.
Okay?
So you have to be engaging and speaking the voice of your attractive, the attractive character when you're using this particular strategy.
Okay?
Like I said, content takes the backseat to entertainment and philosophy.
And the way to think about this two emails a day, I send one in the morning.
Usually goes at around 10 a.m.
I send one afternoon goes at around 4 p.m. every single day.
It breaks my heart not to email.
Now, I'll tell you a real quick reason why.
When I first started this, I said, man, I should take a break on the weekends.
Too much email.
Maybe this is just a little too much.
It was a Sunday afternoon.
I was kind of bored.
I wasn't there in football season.
So I was like, you know, I'm going to, I've got like 400 people on my list.
I just got this affiliate email from, from Derek Gale or internet marketing.
So it's actually Corey back then.
So you know what?
Let me send this email out just to see what happens.
And so the subject on like the truth about women and money or something.
I said, Sunday afternoon.
So I sent it out to a list of 400 people.
Got three sales, 180 bucks.
Oh my God.
Five minutes worth of work and made 180 bucks.
So every time I don't email, I'm losing money.
So I said, well, I hate to lose money.
So I need to send email to these guys every single day.
Two emails a day.
Now, like I said, it's important to think of this is important to do this in the voice of the attractive character.
And it's important to think about it not as email, but as a radio talk show.
Okay.
Because think about it.
Rush Limbaugh, Howard Stern, whoever else is a big radio person.
What do people do deliberately every day?
They got a habit of they go in, they pop their butts down the chair at work, they turn on the radio, and they listen for four hours every single day to this radio personality every single day they listen.
They choose to listen to it.
And some of these guys have very large listenerships.
Okay.
And so if you think of this two emails a day in the context of you're doing a radio show to your customers, it's just broadcast via email.
Then this begins to make a lot more sense.
Because when they get your emails, they say, Oh my God, it's, you know, this guy's, you know, born, meted, ethan, he done subscribe.
It's, Oh, it's time to tune into the radio show.
It's time to tune into the john show today to see what he has to say or not have to say.
Okay.
And so when you look at it in that context, that's when it begins to make sense and becomes and begins to get very, very powerful.
Okay.
Like I said, and you know, the Howard Stern, even Rush Limbaugh, I mean, his little content, mostly entertainment and it's recycled stuff that's over and over and over.
Yet they got audiences that have been with them 10, 15, maybe even 20 years.
Okay.
That tune in deliberately every day to listen to what they have to say.
Now, have you ever listened to Paul Harvey?
Anybody listened to Paul Harvey here?
I mean, I listened to Paul Harvey when I was a kid, you know, love Paul Harvey.
What does Paul Harvey do?
He's on three times a day, right?
Used to be.
Um, but what Paul Harvey does is he used to have the morning news, a little bit more in the afternoon than he'd have his rest of the story.
If you listen to Paul Harvey, when he did, um, his, his afternoon news, he'd always read the news and then it's a page two.

I love, I love, just love to tune into him.

And so what you're doing in the two emails a day is you're mimicking that.
And if people aren't pissed at Paul Harvey, they like Paul Harvey.
Okay.
Paul Harvey, sell him something.
Paul Harvey's getting big fat check to sell him something.
He didn't advertise until but people love it.
Okay.
Because they've tuned in to listen to that.
And so that's what you need to think about this in the context of is that it's like the Paul Harvey radio show, entertainment, news commentary, but also pitch.
Okay.
And the way that I accomplished that is this is how Matt told it to me four years ago and how I still do it to this very day is I do one email in the morning with content and with a soft sell, kind of like Paul Harvey does, which is clearly delineated.
And I do have some samples we are going to go over here.
And then one in the afternoon, which is a hard sell email.
Okay.
It's a short one.
It gets into a website.
It makes them an offer.
And so the way I look at it is this is I gave him something.
Okay.
In the morning, I gave him a good tip.
I gave him some entertainment.
I gave him something with a little bit of a soft sell.
Now in the afternoon, I got permission to sell something to him.
It's a one to one ratio.
I gave him something.
They owe me something.
Okay.
Now I've tried three emails a day.
That doesn't work.
They don't like that.
They'll take two.
Not good enough to be Paul Harvey, but they won't take three.
Okay.
Now one important thing about content in your morning email and that's this, is you want to deliver real content, but you want to deliver such a real narrow slice of that that it leaves them wanting more.
Okay.
That's like one thing I'll teach them is that, you know, hey, here's how to initiate a conversation with a woman.
You do this, this and this.
But what we don't say is we don't talk about how do we get from the next part that we're setting up an initial meeting or throughout that and after that.
They want to know that they got to give them money.
Okay.
But they read that small content.
They say, Hey, wait a second.
This guy, John, he really knows what I have to say.
If I'm walking down the street and I look a woman right in the eye, I smiled at her and she smiles back and I said, What are you smiling about?
She'll actually answer me.
This guy, John is a genius.
Okay.
What do I do next?
Well, you got to give the genius money to find out.
And so that's the thing is that you're giving them enough content wrapped in stories and entertainment and fun, enough content to where they know that you're the real deal.
They just don't have enough actionable information to go out and get results.
I got a lot of guys that are really good going around and get women to smile at them, but they ain't reached out the wallet yet to get to the next step.
Okay.
So they just make a lot of people happy throughout their day.
All right.
But then I got another set that took action and has gone to the next level and went and bought my product.
And so it's a real important thing to include in these two emails a day is just enough specific content to let them know that you're the real deal.
All right.
A couple other interesting things about the strategy and then we'll get into some mechanics.
My Bob was talking today, Rigg Nairus was talking about article marketing and blogs and stuff.
Well, here's a great thing about the two emails a day.
You got articles every single day.
You got blog postings every single day.
So my morning email is put on my johnonliss.com website and becomes an article.
So the search engine can go suck it up and look at it.
I can have other people go out and post these things, articles, different websites.
And so now what you're doing is you're generating a ton of content.
I mean my johnonliss.com website probably has 2000 pages on there.
All right.
Because automatically every day is just updated.
And so what this becomes is you're actually creating an email, a blog post, and an article all at the same time.
And so you're actually getting three times the work for the price of one.
And so when you look at it in that context, it's really not that much work at all.
The other thing about doing this, which is really interesting, is it's like doing a physical workout.
If you do just the 10th, 30, 40 minute workout every day, you stay in great physical shape.
If you go three weeks, you don't do anything.
Trying to get back into it is really hard.
You get sore and you're not as strong, you're not as stamina, and it's terrible.
Well, the same thing applies to being a copywriter.
Okay.
And if you're going to be in this business, it's a good thing to learn the skill of copywriting.
You want to sub it out, but you want to know enough to call BS on some copywriter when they're delivering you garbage instead of delivering you the real deal.
And so what this does is is that when you're writing these relationship building morning emails, and the small lead generation afternoon emails that lead to a pitch, is that you're really honing your copywriting skills.
Because what you're doing is, if you want to get good at writing, you have to write.
And so every morning you're writing something.
And so what this does is it forces you to become a much better copywriter.
Every single day for my afternoon email, I have to come up with a headline that gets the email opened and read.
And it can't be this one as a day before they read it.
So I have to put my mind to work to come up with a headline.
And not only that, I have to do it quickly.
I got a minute, two minutes to come up with it, and that's it.
I don't agonize over it.
I don't think about it.
I do the best I can in 30 seconds a minute to come up with a headline.
And so what this does is, is it forces you to learn how to write copy to time.
All right, there's some copywriters out there that are really good, but they need three months to write a letter.
Three months.
It may be a million dollar letter.
I can write a lot of letters in three months that are almost as good.
Okay.
And so what this does is, is when you're doing this thing, a really neat side benefit of it is, is it forces you to become a good copywriter that writes to time.
Okay.
The other interesting thing about this is that, is you get feedback from your list.
They send things to you, comments and questions.
And I encourage that stuff.
And so you read it.
And so you really get into the heart of what's going on in your list.
You really get to know them intimately.
Okay.
I mean, I know my market very, I know what they want.
I know what they don't want.
And the interesting thing is, is it's much different from what an outsider would think.
All right.
A lot of guys saying, you know, they look at, oh, you're just teaching guys to, you know, chase women.
That's all they're interested in this and that.
That's absolutely not true.
And I know statistically it's not true.
What most guys want is, is they want to meet that one special woman with a few adventures along the way.
What they want.
Statistically, I can tell you that.
All right.
That's reality.
All right.
They don't want to be the guy who's picking up a lot of different women and different women every night for 10 years.
They're not interested in that stuff.
Most of them are.
There may be a small number that are, but 90, 95% when they come to get my stuff, they got that one special woman in mind that's been ignoring them, that they think their life's going to be complete.
Now, we disabuse them of that and teach them, you know, reality.
But there's lots of special women out there guys.
There's not just one.
All right.

What do I meet them?
What do I say?
And so I know I got a lead with that, but then get them excited with everything else that they've told me about the subconscious going on in their minds.
And the only way I was able to figure that out is consistently on a large scale reading the feedback of my readers, surveying them and responding to what they have to say.
And so that when you implement this two emails a day strategy, it forces you to become an excellent copywriter and know more about your market than everyone else that you can dominate it.

I will warn you again about this email stuff.
I like email, but over reliance on online media in your business is a recipe for disaster.
This whole tract of character stuff that I talked about with you pertains to newsletter, tele-seminar, direct mail piece, everything that goes out.
Because online media historically is very perishable.
All right.
You look at infomercials, they were legal in the 50s and they were suddenly illegal.
Then they came back and it was really easy to play in them.
Then the television stations figured out they could charge big bucks for these things.
Okay.
And so the cost of media all of a sudden went up.
Now to play in infomercials, you have to be very sophisticated.
Same thing happened on the internet with Google and paper click.
A lot of opportunity in paper click, but recently all the little people that were making money with AdSense and all this stuff got swept out the door in one day by Google because they just, you're gone.
So anybody this business was dependent on that one media is not having to go get a job.
Okay.
And so, you know, with me, when email goes bad, which it occasionally does, I'm comforted by the fact that I go to my mailbox, I open it and there are checks on the day that the postman is too lazy and decides to go to the donut shop instead of delivering my postcards, it throws them all away.
I go look at my shopping cart and I'm comforted by the fact that I got orders that have come in from online.
Okay.
That I get them by fact.
And so the thing to do is that this is a strategy for protecting your business against disruption is communicating with your customers by a variety of different media using this voice.
Okay.
Email is great.
I love email, but don't become too over-reliant on it.
